summary: Closed unmerged draft/prototype for adding Transmuter workflow support. It added two shell scripts to prepare a Melee single-TU .ctx.c input and compile Transmuter candidates with the Melee mwcc_sjis-style environment, then intentionally broke ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds as a demo target. decomp.dev reported two broken matches and a collaborator asked for resubmission when the tooling was more complete and better explained.

key_files.notes: Runs ninja for build/GALE01/<src>.ctx, copies it to .ctx.c, computes the target object path, supports TRANSMUTER override, and prints a suggested transmuter match command.
Compiles a single preprocessed input to an object with sjiswrap.exe and mwcceppc.exe GC/1.2.5n, Melee-like CFLAGS, include paths, and wibo/wine wrapper selection.
Reordered gm_8016AEEC and gm_8016AF0C calls in ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds; PR body states this was a deliberate break to demo Transmuter.

what_changed: prepare.sh validates that the source exists under src/, derives build/GALE01/... .ctx and build/GALE01/obj/... .o paths, requires the target object to exist, invokes ninja on the .ctx, and copies .ctx to .ctx.c.

prepare.sh prints a node transmuter match invocation using --target, --function, --compiler "tools/transmuter-compile.sh {{inputPath}} {{outputPath}}", --cwd, --isolate, --no-reduce, concurrency, max-iterations, and timeout.

Added tools/transmuter-compile.sh to wrap CodeWarrior compilation for Transmuter candidate files using sjiswrap.exe, mwcceppc.exe, MW_VERSION=GC/1.2.5n, GALE01 defines, Melee include paths, and repo-root cwd.
compile.sh selects wibo on Linux when available, otherwise falls back to wine; non-Linux defaults to wine.
Changed src/melee/if/iftime.c by moving int seconds = gm_8016AEEC() before int centiseconds = gm_8016AF0C() inside ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds.
The PR body explicitly says the ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds change was intentionally breaking the function to demo Transmuter.
decomp.dev reported matched code decreased by 260 bytes with broken matches for ifTime_UpdateCountdown and ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds.
The PR was closed without merge.
smart_moves: Mirrored the project compiler environment instead of using a generic compiler command; the wrapper preserved CodeWarrior version, optimization flags, GALE01 defines, include paths, sjiswrap, and repo-root cwd.
Converted ninja-generated .ctx to .ctx.c so both mwcc and Transmuter accept the preprocessed file extension while leaving the original .ctx reusable.
Computed target object paths from src/ paths, making the prepare helper applicable to any source under src/ rather than hardcoding iftime.c.
Used --isolate in the suggested Transmuter invocation; the script comments state it strips non-target/non-inline bodies and may reduce search iterations by about 30-50% in practice on Melee.
Allowed TRANSMUTER to override the assumed neighboring transmuter checkout path, which avoids hardcoding one developer's filesystem.
Used a small call-order change in ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds as a controlled demo mismatch, although that choice was not suitable for a mergeable PR.
decomp_lessons: Tooling PRs should avoid leaving intentional match regressions in normal source files; use a fixture, branch-only demo, or very clear separation when demonstrating a matcher.
For automated matching tools, candidate compilation must match the project build rule closely: compiler version, flags, defines, include order, sjis handling, and cwd all matter.
A practical Transmuter workflow for Melee starts from ninja-generated build/GALE01/... .ctx, copies it to .ctx.c, and compares against build/GALE01/obj/... .o.
Search-space reduction matters: isolating the target function before mutation can make iterative matching faster and more stable.
Even a small source-order change can break matching, and CI may report breakage in both the edited function and related functions in the same unit.
Reviewer feedback shows that new decomp tooling needs a complete state and clear usage explanation before it is acceptable for resubmission.
naming_conventions: Tool scripts were named with the transmuter- prefix under tools/: transmuter-prepare.sh and transmuter-compile.sh.
The demo followed existing Melee interface naming with ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds and ifTime_ShowTimers.
Undecompiled/address-style game manager symbols retained uppercase hex names: gm_8016AF0C and gm_8016AEEC.
Build artifact naming mapped src/melee/if/iftime.c to build/GALE01/src/melee/if/iftime.ctx, build/GALE01/src/melee/if/iftime.ctx.c, and build/GALE01/obj/melee/if/iftime.o.
Transmuter compiler placeholders used {{inputPath}} and {{outputPath}} in the --compiler string.
The prepare helper used TRANSMUTER as the environment-variable override for the CLI path.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use call/local initialization order as a matching search dimension; this PR's demo mismatch swapped gm_8016AF0C and gm_8016AEEC initialization order.
Compile Transmuter candidates through sjiswrap.exe and mwcceppc.exe rather than bypassing the project's sjis/CodeWarrior wrapper behavior.
Preserve Melee compile flags such as -O4,p, -inline auto, -fp_contract on, -str reuse, -enum int, -proc gekko, and GALE01 build defines when matching generated objects.
Run Transmuter against a preprocessed .ctx.c single translation unit and the extracted target .o object.
Use --isolate to strip unrelated bodies before search; optionally drop --no-reduce if further reducer trimming is desired.
The PR body included a more manual command with --no-cleanup and --seed 42, while prepare.sh's printed command omitted those options; future docs should resolve that discrepancy.
review_feedback: decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code dropped to 66.29%, -0.01% and -260 bytes.
decomp.dev listed two broken matches: main/melee/if/iftime ifTime_UpdateCountdown changed from 100.00% to 85.67%, and ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds changed from 100.00% to 79.00%.
ribbanya commented: "Please resubmit this when it's in a more complete state and with a clearer explanation of its usage."
No inline review comments were present in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find a later resubmission or merged PR that adds Transmuter tooling with complete usage documentation.
Search current master for transmuter-prepare.sh, transmuter-compile.sh, or other Transmuter integration.
Compare any future Transmuter compiler wrapper against the actual mwcc_sjis ninja rule and configure.py wrapper selection.
Check whether ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds remained matched in master after this unmerged demo PR.
Look for later PRs using Transmuter to fix call-order or local-order matching issues in Melee functions.
